DISCOVERY 302, CROSS COMPLAINANTS TEO CHEW COMMUNITY CENTER, INC. AND WILLIAM WONG'S MOTIONS BY DEFT FOR ORDER COMPELLING; 1) FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES; 2) PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS ; 3) FURTHER RESPONSES TO INSPECTION DEMANDS NOS. 3,7 AN D8 AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 6, 2014 TO GIVE THE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN MAY 29, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION AND REPLY PAPERS. PRO TEM JUDGE: ARLENE BARTON; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF TEO CHEW COMMUNITY CENTER, INC.'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. THE COURT MUST EVALUATE TWO FACTORS IN DETERMINING WHETHER TO ISSUE A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION: "(1) THE LIKELIHOOD THAT THE PLAINTIFF WILL PREVAIL ON THE MERITS AT TRIAL AND (2) THE INTERIM HARM THAT THE PLAINTIFF WOULD BE LIKELY TO SUSTAIN IF THE INJUNCTION WERE DENIED AS COMPARED TO THE HARM THE DEFENDANT WOULD BE LIKELY TO SUFFER IF THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION WERE ISSUED." (SMITH V. ADVENTIST HEALTH SYSTEM/WEST (2010) 182 CAL.APP.4TH 729, 749.) THE BURDEN OF PROOF IS ON THE PLAINTIFF. (O'CONNELL V. SUP. CT. (2006) 141 CAL.APP.4TH 1452, 1481.) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET ITS EVIDENTIARY BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS. THE DECLARATION OF LOUIS LAM IS CONCLUSORY AND MANY OF THE STATEMENTS LACK FOUNDATION. FOR EXAMPLE, PLAINTIFF PROVIDES NO COMPETENT EVIDENCE THAT THE BOARD HAD THE AUTHORITY TO DISQUALIFY WILLIAM WONG AS PRESIDENT AND APPOINT LOUIS LAM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR# 8189.

Off Calendar Jul-30-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

Cross Complainants Teo Chew Community Center, Inc. And William Wong'S Motions By Deft For Order Compelling; 1) Further Responses To Interrogatories; 2) Production Of Documents ; 3) Further Responses To Inspection Demands Nos. 3,7 An D8 And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-06-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James W. Moore, Esq. of the Law Office of James W. Moore, Tel. 415.431.3449, appearing specially for defendant and moving party;
Lien L. Uy, Esq. of the Uy Law Group, Tel. 510.893.3294, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: Both parties stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem. After hearing, CROSS COMPLAINANTS TEO CHEW COMMUNITY CENTER, INC. AND WILLIAM WONG'S Motions For Order Compelling 1) Further Responses To Interrogatories; 2) Production Of Documents; 3) Further Responses To Inspection Demands Nos. 3,7 And 8 And For Monetary Sanctions is granted. Parties to prepare and submit a joint proposed order to the Court. Judge Pro Tem: Rebecca Woodson; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
****CROSS COMPLAINANTS TEO CHEW COMMUNITY CENTER, INC. AND WILLIAM WONG'S Motions For Order Compelling 1) Further Responses To Interrogatories; 2) Production Of Documents; 3) Further Responses To Inspection Demands Nos. 3,7 And 8 And For Monetary Sanctions is granted in part and denied in part per Order filed on 06/27/14.****

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Prelim Injunction
Off Calendar Jun-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lien L. Uy, Esq. of Uy Law Group, Tel. 510.893.3294, for plaintiff and moving party;
Christina Y. Chen, Esq. of the Law Offices of Christina Y. Chen, Tel. 415.441.8778, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF TEO CHEW COMMUNITY CENTER, INC.'s Motion For Preliminary Injunction is denied. The Court must evaluate two factors in determining whether to issue a preliminary injunction: "(1) the likelihood that the plaintiff will prevail on the merits at trial and (2) the interim harm that the plaintiff would be likely to sustain if the injunction were denied as compared to the harm the defendant would be likely to suffer if the preliminary injunction were issued." (Smith v. Adventist Health System/West (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 729, 749.) The burden of proof is on the plaintiff. (O'Connell v. Sup. Ct. (2006) 141 Cal.App.4th 1452, 1481.) Plaintiff has not met its evidentiary burden of establishing a likelihood of success on the merits. The Declaration of Louis Lam is conclusory and many of the statements lack foundation. For example, Plaintiff provides no competent evidence that the Board had the authority to disqualify William Wong as president and appoint Louis Lam. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR# 8189.

Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than May 29, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition and reply papers. Pro Tem Judge: Arlene Barton; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/JPT
